title: Ruy Lopez: Marshall Attack
summary: Learn the Marshall Attack, a dangerous theoretical weapon where Black sacrifices a pawn for devastating counterplay.
description: The Marshall Attack is one of the most famous opening surprises. After the normal Ruy Lopez moves, Black sacrifices a pawn for a powerful initiative. You'll learn the main line, the counterattacking ideas, and when to avoid the Marshall versus when to accept it.
line: 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bb5 a6 4.Ba4 Nf6 5.O-O Be7 6.Re1 b5 7.Bb3 d6 8.c3 O-O 9.h3 Na5 10.Bc2 c5 11.d4 Qc7

variations.mainLine: 11...c5 - The Marshall Attack continues with 12.Nbd2 cxd4 13.cxd4
variations.exchange: 8.d4 - The Exchange Variation, also avoids the Marshall

objectives: Understand the Marshall Attack: why Black sacrifices and what they get
Learn the main line moves and the typical tactics
Master White's defensive resources: the forced lines and alternatives
Recognize when the Marshall is playable and when to avoid it
plans: Black's plan: Develop rapidly, attack with ...c5, ...Qc7, ...Re8, and create threats
White must defend precisely or the attack becomes dangerous
The move ...exd4 followed by ...Re8 is a key tactical pattern
In the main line, White often plays a4 to prevent ...b5 and simplify
commonMistakes: Do not underestimate the Marshall - it's a dangerous weapon at all levels
Avoid playing carelessly - the attack is very real
Do not take on e5 too early - this often plays into Black's hands
White should prefer solid development over trying to win the pawn
completionTitle: Black has dangerous initiative for the pawn
completionSummary: The Marshall Attack is a sharp, tactical opening. You've learned the main line and can now handle both sides.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play e4, controlling the center.
steps.coaching: The Marshall Attack occurs in the Ruy Lopez after specific move order.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black plays e5
steps.instruction: Black mirrors in the center.
steps.coaching: The open game begins. The Marshall is coming.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, attacking e5.
steps.coaching: Natural development toward the center.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to c6.
steps.coaching: Black mirrors White's development.

steps.title: The Ruy Lopez
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to b5, pinning the knight.
steps.coaching: This is the Ruy Lopez, one of the most classical openings.

steps.title: Black prevents Bxc6
steps.instruction: The pawn on a6 forces the bishop to retreat.
steps.coaching: This is the Open Sicilian move order applied to the Ruy Lopez.

steps.title: Retreat the bishop
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to a4, maintaining the pin.
steps.coaching: White retreats, keeping the pressure on c6.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6.
steps.coaching: Black continues development. The Marshall is approaching.

steps.title: White castles
steps.instruction: Castle kingside, getting the king to safety.
steps.coaching: White castles early, a sign of confidence against the Marshall.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to e7.
steps.coaching: Black prepares to castle and is setting up the Marshall.

steps.title: Support the center
steps.instruction: Move the rook to e1, supporting e4.
steps.coaching: This move prepares for the Marshall - but also defends against it.

steps.title: The Marshall preparation
steps.instruction: Black pushes b5, preparing the sacrifice.
steps.coaching: This move prepares the Marshall Attack. Black is about to sacrifice.

steps.title: White avoids the trap
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to b3, avoiding the c4 square.
steps.coaching: White plays Bb3 to avoid the tactical pitfalls of the Marshall.

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: The pawn on d6 supports ...c5 and development.
steps.coaching: Black is setting up the standard Marshall pawn structure.

steps.title: Support the d4 square
steps.instruction: Play c3 to support d4.
steps.coaching: White prepares d4 while defending against Black's central break.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.instruction: Black castles kingside.
steps.coaching: Both sides are now castled. The Marshall is about to begin.

steps.title: Prevent Black's trick
steps.instruction: Play h3, preventing ...Ng4 and preparing development.
steps.coaching: This move prevents the immediate Marshall but allows the main line.

steps.title: Black attacks the bishop
steps.instruction: The knight attacks c4, forcing the bishop to move.
steps.coaching: This is the Marshall move order. Black forces Bc2.

steps.title: Retreat the bishop
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to c2.
steps.coaching: White retreats, completing development.

steps.title: The Marshall Advance
steps.instruction: Black pushes c5, opening the position.
steps.coaching: This is the key Marshall move! Black sacrifices a pawn for the attack.

steps.title: White accepts the gambit
steps.instruction: Play d4 to challenge the center.
steps.coaching: White accepts the pawn. Now both sides must play precisely.
steps.hint: Push the d-pawn to challenge c5.

steps.title: Black exchanges
steps.instruction: Black captures on d4.
steps.coaching: Black gets the pawn back but keeps the initiative.

steps.title: White recaptures
steps.instruction: Take back with the c-pawn.
steps.coaching: White maintains a slight material advantage but must defend.

steps.title: Black develops the queen
steps.instruction: The queen moves to c7, supporting the attack.
steps.coaching: The queen joins the attack. White must be careful.
